2012-09-01.title: Pakistan’s Premier Televangelist
2012-09-01.abstract: Aamir Liaquat Hussain is a broadcasting sensation and a contentious figure.

2012-09-01.title: Cardinal Carlo Martini, Papal Contender, Dies at 85
2012-09-01.abstract: Cardinal Martini, one of the Roman Catholic Church’s most influential progressive thinkers, was considered a possible successor to Pope John Paul II.

2012-09-01.title: A Superstar Televangelist in Pakistan Divides, Then Repents
2012-09-01.abstract: Aamir Liaquat Hussain, who has been accused of using his television pulpit to promote hate speech, now portrays himself as a torchbearer for progressive values.

2012-09-01.title: Rage by Miners Points to Shift in South Africa
2012-09-01.abstract: The unrest at South African mines has placed the spotlight on the country’s business leaders and politicians, who once led protests and are now wealthy members of the establishment.

2012-09-01.title: Mexico: Drug Gang Suspect Extradited to the U.S.
2012-09-01.abstract: A 20-year investigation into one of Mexico’s largest drug trafficking organizations came to a close Friday when Eduardo Arellano-Félix, a top operative of the gang, was extradited to San Diego.

2012-09-02.title: Clinton Begins Asia Trip, Trying to Ease Tension With China
2012-09-02.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton began a six-nation visit to Asia by saying that the United States would broaden its presence in the region, but that “the Pacific is big enough for all of us.”

2012-09-02.title: Kurt Maetzig, Pioneering German Director, Dies at 101
2012-09-02.abstract: Mr. Maetzig, a pioneering figure in East Germany’s socialist film industry after World War II, helped postwar Germany come to terms with its Nazi past.

2012-09-02.title: Campaign References to Afghanistan Are Missing In Action
2012-09-02.abstract: With the exception of that odd Clint Eastwood interlude, the war in Afghanistan and the U.S. troops fighting there were barely mentioned onstage at the Republican convention — and not at all in Mitt Romney’s acceptance speech. Will the Democrats, perhaps sensing an opening, make the war more of an issue at their convention this week?

2012-09-02.title: U.S. Halts Training Program for Some Afghan Recruits
2012-09-02.abstract: Training of Afghan Local Police and special operations forces has been put on hold to better screen out threats to troops, officials say.

2012-09-02.title: Has the ‘Burqa Ban’ Worked in France?
2012-09-02.abstract: The French law banning the full-face veil from public spaces has been controversial from the start, with loud debates about the meaning of liberty, individual rights, the freedoms of religion and expression, and the nature of laïcité, or secularism, in the French republic.

2012-09-03.title: Egypt Abuzz as Newsreader on State TV Wears Hijab
2012-09-03.abstract: The appearance is said to be the first of a veiled news presenter on state television, which had enforced a de facto ban during the Mubarak era.

2012-09-03.title: Pakistani Blasphemy Case Shifts as Cleric Is Arrested
2012-09-03.abstract: The Muslim cleric who had expressed anger at a Christian girl’s alleged desecration of sacred Muslim texts has been accused by a colleague of falsifying evidence.

2012-09-03.title: A Growing Nuclear Program
2012-09-03.abstract: Iran has been developing its nuclear enrichment program since 1985.

2012-09-03.title: To Calm Israel, U.S. Offers Ways to Restrain Iran
2012-09-03.abstract: The Obama administration is taking steps it hopes will force Iran to reconsider almost stalemated nuclear negotiations — and forestall an Israeli attack on Iran’s nuclear facilities.

2012-09-03.title: Mexico City’s Aztec Past Reaches Out to Present
2012-09-03.abstract: Mexico City might be one of the world’s classic megacities, but it is also an archaeological wonder, with surprises still being uncovered in the city’s superimposed layers.

2012-09-04.title: Northern Ireland: Protesters Injure Police Officers in Belfast
2012-09-04.abstract: The police said that 47 police officers were injured Sunday when Protestant protesters angered by a Catholic parade in northern Belfast threw homemade bombs, bricks and fireworks.

2012-09-04.title: Syrian Children Offer Glimpse of a Future of Reprisals
2012-09-04.abstract: If Syrian rebel fighters portray their battle as a struggle for democracy, refugee children at the Zaatari camp in Jordan tell a much uglier story of sectarian revenge against Alawites.

2012-09-04.title: Disputed Islands in the South China Sea
2012-09-04.abstract: Disputes between China and its neighbors over potentially energy-rich islands have escalated in recent months.

2012-09-04.title: South Africa: Striking Miners Hurt in Clash With Police
2012-09-04.abstract: The police used rubber bullets to disperse a crowd of striking gold miners on Monday at the Gold Fields mine, just east of Johannesburg, injuring four of them.

2012-09-04.title: Illegal Ivory Trade
2012-09-04.abstract: Most of the illegal ivory seized in large-scale shipments in the past three years originated in Kenya and Tanzania.

2012-09-05.title: Colombia Will Restart Peace Process With FARC
2012-09-05.abstract: President Juan Manuel Santos and the leader of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia said that the two sides would soon sit down to a new phase of peace talks.

2012-09-05.title: Iran Supplying Syrian Military via Iraqi Airspace
2012-09-05.abstract: Iran has resumed shipping military equipment to Syria over Iraqi airspace, showing the limits of American influence in the region after the White House pressed Baghdad to close the corridor.

2012-09-05.title: Two Hungry Nations Collide Over Fishing
2012-09-05.abstract: A fishing war is straining relations between India and Sri Lanka, as Indian fishermen, often poor and desperate, regularly cross international waters and run afoul of the Sri Lankan Navy.

2012-09-05.title: Israel: Trappist Monastery Is Vandalized
2012-09-05.abstract: Vandals burned the door of a Trappist monastery at Latrun, near Jerusalem, early Tuesday and scrawled anti-Christian slogans on the walls.

2012-09-05.title: Mexico: Major Drug Suspect Is Arrested
2012-09-05.abstract: Mario Cárdenas Guillén, a presumed top leader of one of the country’s largest and most powerful criminal groups, was caught by the Mexican Navy on Monday as he was carrying a large gun.

2012-09-06.title: Germany: Officials in Berlin Clarify Guidelines on Circumcision
2012-09-06.abstract: To create legal clarity since a court ruling in June equated circumcision with bodily injury, justice officials in Berlin laid out guidelines to allow the practice to continue in the city.

2012-09-06.title: Russia: Police Convoy Is Attacked in Restive Ingushetia
2012-09-06.abstract: Five people died and at least one was wounded when a police convoy came under attack on Wednesday in the volatile southern Russian republic of Ingushetia.

2012-09-06.title: Leak Prompts Netanyahu to Cancel Security Cabinet Meeting on Iran Issue
2012-09-06.abstract: Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, angrily cut short a key gathering of top security officials, accusing an unnamed participant of leaking details of the sensitive meeting to the media.

2012-09-06.title: Gaza: Israeli Airstrike Kills Three Militants
2012-09-06.abstract: An Israeli airstrike killed three Palestinian militants in the Gaza Strip late Wednesday and seriously wounded a fourth, according to Palestinian and Israeli officials.

2012-09-06.title: U.S. Presses Iraq on Iranian Planes Thought to Carry Arms to Syria
2012-09-06.abstract: The United States said Wednesday that Iraq had an obligation to stop and inspect Iranian planes flying through its airspace that are suspected of carrying arms to Syria.

2012-09-07.title: Russia: Pussy Riot Lawyer Questioned
2012-09-07.abstract: The police summoned a lawyer for the punk band Pussy Riot for questioning in a criminal case that the authorities say is unrelated to the Pussy Riot case.

2012-09-07.title: Russia: Fighter Jet Crashes, Killing Pilot
2012-09-07.abstract: A Russian Air Force MiG-29 fighter jet crashed Thursday afternoon near Chita, a city in Eastern Siberia, slamming into a hill close to a local airport and killing the pilot.

2012-09-07.title: Assassination Highlights Rifts Facing Syria Rebels
2012-09-07.abstract: The killing of a rebel fighter linked to Al Qaeda called new attention to the ideological differences among rebels fighting the government of President Bashar al-Assad.

2012-09-07.title: Israeli Soldiers Kill 3 Palestinians Near Gaza Security Fence
2012-09-07.abstract: Israel said that the men were planting a bomb; a Gazan official said the men died after ambulances came under fire and were delayed.

2012-09-07.title: In Afghanistan, Avoiding Violent Misunderstandings Among Troops
2012-09-07.abstract: A tense exchange at a news conference highlighted some of the challenges NATO faces in working with Afghans troops in the wake of internal attacks against Western soldiers.

2012-09-08.title: A Writer Evokes Loss on South Korea’s Path to Success
2012-09-08.abstract: Shin Kyung-sook, one of South Korea’s most acclaimed and successful writers, focuses on social upheaval and the price of her country’s success in novels like “Please Look After Mom.”

2012-09-08.title: From Israel’s Archives, Papers on Munich Killings
2012-09-08.abstract: Dozens of classified documents relating to the 1972 killing of Olympic athletes in Germany have been made public after sitting for four decades in cardboard boxes at the Israel State Archives.

2012-09-08.title: From Politics to Ads, Israel Archives Meet YouTube
2012-09-08.abstract: In line with a recent policy of giving the public as much access as possible to historical material, the country’s national archivists have taken to relying on YouTube.

2012-09-08.title: Canada Closes Tehran Embassy and Orders Iran Envoys to Leave
2012-09-08.abstract: Calling Iran “the most significant threat to global peace and security in the world today,” Canada’s foreign affairs minister said his government had cut all diplomatic ties with the country.

2012-09-08.title: Said K. Aburish, Palestinian Journalist, Dies at 77
2012-09-08.abstract: Mr. Aburish, a former arms dealer with a Harvard M.B.A., skewered Arab leaders in his 11 books.

2012-09-09.title: Taliban Rages Over U.S. Decision on Terrorist Group
2012-09-09.abstract: When the United States last week declared the Haqqani network in Afghanistan to be a terrorist organization, the Taliban’s reaction was angry, swift and deadly. Noting the close ties between the Haqqanis and Pakistan’s main spy agency, some analysts saw a possibility that Pakistan itself might some day be declared a state sponsor of terrorism.

2012-09-09.title: The ‘Control Freaks’ Behind the Wheel
2012-09-09.abstract: Lewis Hamilton and Jenson Button were the fastest drivers during qualifying on Saturday for the Italian Grand Prix. The relatively flat course at Monza, unlike the wet, hilly and sometimes scary track at Spa last week, is the fastest of the season.

2012-09-09.title: Death Sentence for a Top Iraqi Leader in a Day of Bloodshed
2012-09-09.abstract: Tariq al-Hashimi, the Iraqi vice president accused of commanding sectarian death squads, was sentenced hours after a wave of attacks that claimed at least 100 lives.

2012-09-09.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-09-09.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-09-09.title: Syria Criticizes France for Supporting Rebels, as Fears Grow of Islamist Infiltration
2012-09-09.abstract: Days after the French government said that it would provide assistance directly to Syrian rebels, Syria accused France of undermining the new United Nations envoy.

2012-09-10.title: U.S. Puts Transfer of Detainees to Afghans on Hold
2012-09-10.abstract: While American concerns halted one of the requirements for switching control of the Parwan prison, Afghanistan vowed to go on with a ceremony marking its takeover.

2012-09-10.title: German Envoy Goes to Greece, Leaving Lectures Behind
2012-09-10.abstract: Angela Merkel, the German chancellor, has sent Hans-Joachim Fuchtel, a jovial, brass-band-loving Black Forester, to Greece to help mend frayed relations.

2012-09-10.title: Pro-Beijing Candidates Outmaneuver Opponents as Hong Kong Votes
2012-09-10.abstract: Voters turned out in big numbers after a controversy over a government plan to introduce mandatory pro-Beijing patriotic education in Hong Kong schools.

2012-09-10.title: Fighting for Bangladesh Labor, and Ending Up in Pauper’s Grave
2012-09-10.abstract: The murder of a labor organizer bore a grim familiarity in a country with a brutal legacy of politically motivated killings.

2012-09-10.title: U.N. Report Questions Gaza’s Future
2012-09-10.abstract: The United Nations questioned whether Gaza will be “a liveable place” in 2020, citing shortages of important services.

2012-09-11.title: Obama Nominates Envoy to Iraq
2012-09-11.abstract: President Obama said he would nominate Robert Stephen Beecroft, a career diplomat, as the next ambassador to Iraq after his first choice, Brett H. McGurk, was forced to withdraw.

2012-09-11.title: Ecuadoreans Close Ranks in Assange Standoff
2012-09-11.abstract: While some Ecuadoreans have only a vague idea of who Julian Assange is, they are clear in their pleasure that their country stood up to Britain in the asylum dispute.

2012-09-11.title: Verghese Kurien, Leader of India’s Milk Cooperatives, Dies at 90
2012-09-11.abstract: Mr. Kurien helped thousands of dairy farmers create cooperatives to produce and market milk, reducing hunger and poverty and eventually transforming India into the world’s largest milk producer.

2012-09-11.title: Amputations and Killings Shake an Embattled Mali
2012-09-11.abstract: Radical Islamists in northern Mali cut off the hands and feet of four robbery suspects, while in southern Mali, the government was on the defensive after the army killed 16 preachers.

2012-09-11.title: A Dictator Is Gone, but Egypt’s Traffic and Congestion Seem Immovable
2012-09-11.abstract: Street vendors, erratic drivers, nonchalant traffic police officers: all contribute to a chaos in Cairo that is estimated to cost as much as $8 billion in lost productivity, delays and fuel waste.

2012-09-12.title: Putin Impedes European Inquiry of State Fuel Supplier
2012-09-12.abstract: A decree issued by President Vladimir V. Putin prevents Russian companies like the natural gas giant Gazprom from providing foreign regulators with information without Kremlin approval.

2012-09-12.title: Spain: Marchers Demand Independence for Catalonia, as Budget Cuts Bolster Separatism
2012-09-12.abstract: Hundreds of thousands, waving flags and carrying signs, rallied in Barcelona on Tuesday for independence for the region of Catalonia.

2012-09-12.title: France: Former Premier Is Questioned in Fraud Case
2012-09-12.abstract: Gendarmes questioned Dominique de Villepin on Tuesday on suspicion of involvement in fraud that investigators believe was committed by a friend, the businessman Régis Bulot.

2012-09-12.abstract: British schools now do a better job of lifting students up the social ladder, the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development said in a report published Tuesday.

2012-09-12.title: Gaza: Bulldozers Raze Part of Forgotten Neighborhood
2012-09-12.abstract: The Hamas government demolished more than a dozen makeshift homes this week in the impoverished enclave, and officials said they would soon remove the rest of the structures.

2012-09-13.title: The Country Beckons Spaniards as Jobs in Cities Grow Scarce
2012-09-13.abstract: For generations, Spaniards have left their homes in the country to seek jobs in the city, but now many are moving back.

2012-09-13.title: In South Africa, Labor Unrest in Mining Deepens
2012-09-13.abstract: A platinum producer suspended operations, and tear gas was fired at protesting workers at a gold mine in signs that spreading turmoil is destabilizing a crucial industry.

2012-09-13.title: Attack Victim Shared His Fears in Messages
2012-09-13.abstract: Sean Smith, an Air Force veteran working as an information management officer in Libya, was one of the four Americans killed in Tuesday’s attack on the diplomatic mission in Benghazi.

2012-09-13.title: Struggle for Ideological Upper Hand in Muslim World Seen as Factor in Attacks
2012-09-13.abstract: Some analysts see the protests as less about Muslim intolerance than about extremists’ exploiting popular anger to draw attention to political goals.

2012-09-13.title: Spotlight Is on Libya, but Bigger Challenge for White House May Lie in Egypt
2012-09-13.abstract: The Egyptian government’s response to violence at the American Embassy in Cairo gave the Obama administration further cause for concern.

2012-09-14.abstract: Defense Secretary Leon Panetta will outline how the Pentagon is refocusing on the Pacific, as the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan are over or winding down.

2012-09-14.abstract: For Glen Doherty, killed Tuesday in the attack on a diplomatic compound in Libya, being assigned to a high-stakes security detail was just one of many adventurous overseas missions.

2012-09-14.abstract: If and when China’s presumptive new leader, Xi Jinping, re-emerges, he will do so in the midst of a contentious Communist Party preparing to shake up its leadership.

2012-09-14.abstract: The mayhem that killed and wounded more than a dozen American officials was actually two attacks — the first spontaneous and the second organized, a top Libyan security official said.

2012-09-14.title: Egypt, Hearing From Obama, Moves to Heal Rift From Protests
2012-09-14.abstract: A blunt phone call from President Obama prompted Egyptian leaders to scramble Thursday to try to repair the country’s alliance with Washington.

2012-09-15.abstract: Ambassador J. Christopher Stevens reportedly took shelter in a hot, smoke-filled room and lost consciousness there during the attack on the diplomatic compound in Benghazi, Libya.

2012-09-15.title: South Africa Warns Against Violence as Protests Fester
2012-09-15.abstract: Amid calls for a national miners’ strike, South Africa’s justice minister said the unrest would dent the country’s image and hurt its economy.

2012-09-15.title: After Life in New York, Banker Returns to India for Turn at Fashion
2012-09-15.abstract: When an investment banker came back from the United States, she needed to remake her wardrobe. Now, she manages brand strategy for a leading Indian fashion house.

2012-09-15.title: The American Diplomatic Compound in Benghazi
2012-09-15.abstract: Libyans found the body of J. Christopher Stevens in a safe room in the American Diplomatic Compound in Benghazi.

2012-09-15.title: Google Has No Plans to Rethink Video Status
2012-09-15.abstract: Google said it would not comply with a White House request to reconsider the anti-Islam video that has set off violent protests, saying it did not violate its terms of service regarding hate speech.

2012-09-16.abstract: Tissue engineers have succeeded in making artificial organs that use a patient’s cells to become a living part of the body, with hope for eventual organ regeneration.

2012-09-16.abstract: Yukio Edano, the minister for economy, trade and industry, cast further doubt on whether the resource-poor nation will follow through on a contentious plan to phase out nuclear power.

2012-09-16.abstract: India’s minister of commerce and industry explains recent reforms.

2012-09-16.abstract: The assault late Friday on one of the best-defended posts in Afghanistan was troubling because the attackers were able to penetrate the base, killing two and causing more than $200 million in damage.

2012-09-16.abstract: A former human rights lawyer and close ally of the late President Roh Moo-hyun was chosen Sunday as the main opposition Democratic United Party’s candidate for December’s election.

2012-09-17.abstract: President Hamid Karzai of Afghanistan condemned American-led forces for killing eight women in airstrikes and denounced his American allies for continuing to hold Afghan prisoners.

2012-09-17.abstract: Do strange nicknames confer an electoral advantage? Some candidates for office in Brazil, including Ladi Gaga, Christ of Jerusalem and several Batmans, seem to think so.

2012-09-17.abstract: An amateur video appears to show a crowd removing the motionless body of Ambassador J. Christopher Stevens from a window of the American mission in Benghazi.

2012-09-17.abstract: On the final day of his trip to Lebanon, Pope Benedict XVI said a prayer for the victims of the conflict in Syria and appealed to the region and the world to find a solution.

2012-09-17.title: Mideast Unrest Intensifies Debate on U.S. Intervention in Syria
2012-09-17.abstract: The attacks on American and European diplomatic posts in recent weeks have roused calls for disengagement from the Arab world.

2012-09-18.title: Israeli Diplomat Is Man in Middle
2012-09-18.abstract: Ambassador Michael B. Oren, who happens to have been born and bred in New Jersey, is representing a prime minister who has infuriated the White House.

2012-09-18.title: Iranian Official Says Blasts Targeted Nuclear Sites
2012-09-18.abstract: Iran’s top atomic energy official said that explosions were intended to cut power to two uranium enrichment facilities and claimed saboteurs had infiltrated an international inspection agency.

2012-09-18.abstract: The Polish government has decided that the Wolf’s Lair, the outpost where Hitler commanded the Third Reich for years, holds valuable history lessons that need to be preserved.

2012-09-18.abstract: A Hamas military court on Monday convicted four Palestinians of taking part in the 2011 kidnapping and killing of a pro-Palestinian Italian activist in the Gaza Strip.

2012-09-18.title: In Car Country, Obama Trumpets China Trade Case
2012-09-18.abstract: President Obama on Monday filed a broad new trade case against China at the World Trade Organization. He announced the action in the industrial battleground of Ohio.

2012-09-19.title: Race Is On as Ice Melt Reveals Arctic Treasures
2012-09-19.abstract: The jockeying among nations has begun as areas of the Arctic once regarded as barren wastelands now offer an abundance of oil, gas and minerals.

2012-09-19.title: Britain: Prince Loses Ruling on Letters
2012-09-19.abstract: A tribunal in London ruled that letters written by Prince Charles lobbying government ministers on a range of contentious political issues must be made public within a month.

2012-09-19.title: Suzy Menkes: London Is on a Fashion Roll
2012-09-19.abstract: Holly Fulton. Henry Holland. Daniella Issa Helayel. Christopher Raeburn. Marios Schwab. Esthetica. A taste for sustainability. A penchant for experimentation. What sets London fashion apart.

2012-09-19.title: 131 Escape in One of Mexico’s Largest Jailbreaks
2012-09-19.abstract: Prison officials are under suspicion after the inmates escaped through a tunnel in Piedras Negras.

2012-09-19.title: After Libya, Renewed Questions About the Potency of Al Qaeda
2012-09-19.abstract: The attack in Benghazi has set off a new debate in Washington and across the Middle East about whether Al Qaeda has been reinvigorated or lives on as a useful boogeyman.

2012-09-20.title: Advocate of Democracy in Myanmar Meets Obama
2012-09-20.abstract: Daw Aung San Suu Kyi called for sanctions against Myanmar to be eased, saying they had served their purpose.

2012-09-20.title: Santiago Carrillo, a Spanish Communist Leader, Dies at 97
2012-09-20.abstract: Mr. Carrillo fought Gen. Francisco Franco’s fascist forces during the Spanish Civil War, and later promoted a new, more moderate Communism.

2012-09-20.title: High Court in Italy Backs Convictions for Rendition
2012-09-20.abstract: If extradited, 22 C.I.A. employees and an Air Force colonel could face prison in the 2003 abduction of the Islamic militant known as Abu Omar.

2012-09-20.abstract: Mr. Zuno was a central defendant in the 1985 torture and killing of Enrique Camarena Salazar, an American drug enforcement agent, in Mexico.

2012-09-20.title: Ex-Commander in Afghanistan Eyed for Africa
2012-09-20.abstract: In the wake of the Arab Spring, the Africa Command is seen as one of the most challenging theaters of operation.

2012-09-21.title: Violence Over Video Continues in Pakistan Ahead of Public Holiday
2012-09-21.abstract: Thousands demonstrated in the capital, Islamabad, on the eve of a public holiday to protest an anti-Islam video, while the United States Embassy broadcast ads showing American leaders denouncing the video.

2012-09-21.title: The Attack on the American Mission in Benghazi, Libya
2012-09-21.abstract: Details on the attack on Sept. 11, 2012, that killed the American ambassador to Libya.

2012-09-21.title: For Scandal-Weary Italians, Another Scandal
2012-09-21.abstract: Italians who thought they had seen everything when it came to politics were taken aback when photographs were published of regional officials having a “return of Ulysses” party.

2012-09-21.title: Territorial Disputes Involving Japan
2012-09-21.abstract: Territorial disputes between Japan and its neighbors have escalated in recent months, prompting protests and diplomatic tensions.

2012-09-21.title: After Attack in Libya, an Ambush Struck Rescuers
2012-09-21.abstract: The assault on American diplomatic facilities in Benghazi was moblike at first but became a sophisticated onslaught.

2012-09-22.title: A Reporter Meets His Censor
2012-09-22.abstract: It was a surreal, Wizard of Oz moment — meeting the Burmese censor of my articles in the International Herald Tribune. I had expected to peer behind the curtain and see an ill-tempered older man with thick glasses, a green eyeshade and a permanent scowl. I was wrong.

2012-09-22.title: Jesse Jackson Helps Free 2 From Gambia Prison
2012-09-22.abstract: Gambia’s president, Yahya Jammeh, freed the men, former officials in his government, after the Rev. Jesse Jackson visited and made a personal plea.

2012-09-22.abstract: An Egyptian magazine published what it said was an exclusive interview with President Bashar al-Assad in which he criticized Turkey, Qatar and Saudi Arabia for supporting his government’s opponents.

2012-09-22.abstract: The humanitarian and security situation in northern Mali has eroded under Al Qaeda and the other extremist groups controlling the region, the United Nations Security Council said Friday.

2012-09-22.title: Russia: Dogs Poisoned in Moscow
2012-09-22.abstract: The Moscow police are searching for a band of vigilante “dog hunters” who have set traps with poisoned meat to kill stray dogs. But the poison has killed pets instead.

2012-09-23.title: In U.S. Exit From Iraq, Failed Efforts and Challenges
2012-09-23.abstract: The United States’ goal has been to leave a stable government, avoid a power vacuum and maintain influence, but the Obama administration has fallen short on some objectives.

2012-09-23.abstract: It’s unlikely that Japan, China and Taiwan would agree among themselves to relinquish their claims to some remote and barren islands, but there are several international courts, tribunals and treaties that could sort things out.

2012-09-23.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-09-23.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-09-23.title: In Praise of What Has Been Lost at U.S. Embassies
2012-09-23.abstract: Embassies used to be essential places for journalists and diplomats to trade real information. All that changed even before the Chris Stevens tragedy.

2012-09-23.abstract: The Chinese authorities announced the guilty verdict for Wang Lijun, who exposed the slaying of a British businessman by a senior politician’s wife.

2012-09-24.title: Government Issues Order to Disband Libya Forces
2012-09-24.abstract: Libya’s interim government ordered the breakup of all militias that do not fall under its authority, and demanded that those militias pull out of military compounds and public property within 48 hours.

2012-09-24.title: Discovery of Skeleton Puts Richard III in Battle Once Again
2012-09-24.abstract: Supporters who believe Richard III’s reputation was smeared hope the potential discovery of his skeleton will lead to a reassessment of his brief but violent reign.

2012-09-24.abstract: Among personnel pulled from Benghazi after the assault on the American mission were intelligence operatives and contractors who were crucial to surveillance on militants.

2012-09-24.title: At United Nations, Renewed Focus on Syria, if Not New Ideas
2012-09-24.abstract: Despite at least three high-level meetings planned during the General Assembly, no broad new initiatives to end the war are expected.

2012-09-24.title: Sinai Group Claims Role in Attack on Israel
2012-09-24.abstract: An obscure militant group based in the Sinai region claimed responsibility for a cross-border attack Friday that killed an Israeli soldier.

2012-09-25.title: Italy: Regional Leader Resigns
2012-09-25.abstract: Renata Polverini, the president of the Lazio region, which includes Rome, resigned Monday in the wake of a political finance scandal that includes accusations of the misuse of public money.

2012-09-25.title: Two Marines Face Charges
2012-09-25.abstract: The Marine Corps said Monday that two noncommissioned officers accused of urinating on the bodies of dead Taliban fighters in Afghanistan last year would be court-martialed.

2012-09-25.title: India: Deluge Strikes Northeast
2012-09-25.abstract: While parts of India struggle with crop failures and water shortages, the northeastern states of Sikkim and Assam have been hit by heavy rains and flash floods that have caused landslides and claimed at least 27 lives in recent days.

2012-09-25.abstract: Georgian officials sought to take control of a week-old prison abuse scandal, arresting three activists from an opposition party.

2012-09-25.title: Before Palestinians Act at U.N., Israeli Officials Urge Other Steps
2012-09-25.abstract: Dan Meridor, the deputy prime minister, emphasized the need to return to the negotiating table, while Defense Minister Ehud Barak called for annexation of three large settlement blocs.

2012-09-26.title: Maurice H. Keen Dies at 78; Redefined Chivalry
2012-09-26.abstract: Mr. Keen was a scholar who re-examined the code used among knights and determined it was meant to protect the warrior class rather than to emphasize good deeds.

2012-09-26.title: Russia: 2 Police Officers Found Guilty in Police Brutality Case in Kazan
2012-09-26.abstract: A regional court handed down the first guilty verdicts in a case that exposed the systematic torture of suspects in a police department in the capital of the Tatarstan Republic.

2012-09-26.title: As Libya Presses Some Militias to Disband, Fighters Attack a Hotel in Tripoli
2012-09-26.abstract: The attack on a hotel housing newly elected lawmakers came as an American team arrived in Tripoli to investigate the death of Ambassador J. Christopher Stevens.

2012-09-26.title: In Afghanistan, American Trainers Keep Their Armor On
2012-09-26.abstract: In the field, where small teams of American advisers are now working with Afghan units, even minor misunderstandings are treated as potentially violent confrontations.

2012-09-26.title: Training Afghan Allies, With Guard Firmly Up
2012-09-26.abstract: After recent attacks by Afghan forces against Western soldiers and Marines, American military advisers are extremely wary, laying bare the challenge of preparing the Afghans to fight on their own.

2012-09-27.title: Political Shifts in Bavaria, Land of Tradition, Could Have Wide Repercussions
2012-09-27.abstract: Bavaria’s traditional culture may be falling victim to its economic success, as the governing party’s philosophy is tested by newcomers.

2012-09-27.title: As Power Flows to Regional Bosses, Questions Rise on India’s Economy
2012-09-27.abstract: India’s political calculus is again in flux, with the economy in a tailspin and power radiating to regional political chieftains, who are considering a new national political alignment.

2012-09-27.title: Rebels Make Gains in Blunting Syrian Air Attacks
2012-09-27.abstract: For the rebels, managing to deny the use of an important airfield has undermined the government’s ability to exert its full authority in some parts of the country.

2012-09-27.title: Tensions Intensify in Greece
2012-09-27.abstract: Clashes erupted in central Athens and across Greece on Wednesday as trade unions called a nationwide strike to contest new salary and pension cuts being discussed by the government and its international creditors.

2012-09-27.title: Cyberwarfare Emerges From Shadows for Public Discussion by U.S. Officials
2012-09-27.abstract: The federal government’s announcement of a program to enlist contractors to develop technology is seen as a turning point in a long-secret program.

2012-09-28.title: Iran Envoy Is Assaulted by Protesters Near the U.N.
2012-09-28.abstract: A spokesman for the Iranian Mission at the United Nations was shoved and shouted at by a small group of Iranian exiles protesting a speech at the General Assembly by Iran’s president.

2012-09-28.title: Administration Statements on the Attack in Benghazi
2012-09-28.abstract: Statements made by officials in the Obama administration about the Sept. 11, 2012, attack on the mission in Benghazi, Libya.

2012-09-28.title: Man Tied to Anti-Islam Video Held on Probation Charge
2012-09-28.abstract: The man suspected of making the anti-Islam video that set off deadly protests across the Muslim world is accused of violating an order in a bank fraud case that restricted his use of the Internet.

2012-09-28.title: Syrian Rebels Take Their Battle to the Skies
2012-09-28.abstract: For the rebels, managing to deny the use of an important airfield has undermined the government’s ability to exert its full authority in some parts of the country.

2012-09-28.title: Challenging Crossings
2012-09-28.abstract: Thomas Fuller, a New York Times reporter, visits Vietnam’s capital to document the daily duel between motorcycles and pedestrians.

2012-09-29.title: Russian Parliament Bars Opposition Lawmaker From Speaking
2012-09-29.abstract: Russia’s Parliament voted Friday to bar Ilya V. Ponomarev from speaking on the floor for one month after he called members of the majority party “swindlers and thieves.”

2012-09-29.title: Colombia Absolves Kidnapping Victim Accused of Misdeeds
2012-09-29.abstract: The authorities said Sigifredo López, a former state legislator suspected of helping to arrange his own kidnapping by rebels in 2002, had been wrongly accused.

2012-09-29.title: At Annual U.N. General Assembly, a Stage for All the World
2012-09-29.abstract: At the annual session, those with global issues to press — no matter how tough the sell — worked to get their messages across to an international audience of leaders.

2012-09-29.title: Human Faces of ‘The Backlog’ at the Department of Veterans Affairs
2012-09-29.abstract: A crushing inventory of claims for disability, pension and educational benefits has overwhelmed the Department of Veterans Affairs. For some of the people behind the claims, the long wait has been too long.

2012-09-29.title: The Growing Role of Kurds in Syria
2012-09-29.abstract: The Syrian government gave control of some areas to local Kurdish militias in exchange for their support.

2012-09-30.title: Elite and Deft, Xi Aimed High Early in China
2012-09-30.abstract: An early move to rural China offers a window on the political savvy of Xi Jinping, who is on the cusp of taking over as China’s supreme leader.

2012-09-30.title: Japanese Novelist Denounces ‘Cheap Liquor’ of Nationalism
2012-09-30.abstract: The Japanese novelist Haruki Murakami, listed by oddsmakers as the favorite to win the 2012 Nobel Prize in literature, has called for Japan to reflect on its historical legacy in light of heated disputes with China and South Korea. He also denounced a rising nationalism in the region as “cheap liquor” supplied by “politicians and polemicists.”

2012-09-30.title: 5 Are Dead After Clash Between U.S. and Afghan Troops
2012-09-30.abstract: Two Americans and three Afghans died just days after Pentagon officials confirmed that joint operations between American and Afghan forces were returning to normal.

2012-09-30.title: Bo Xilai’s Son Defends His ‘Upright’ Father
2012-09-30.abstract: Bo Guagua, the son of the disgraced former Communist Party leader Bo Xilai, has posted a statement online about the “upright” father whom he knew to be “devoted to duty.” The elder Bo, expelled last Friday from the party, is due to stand trial on criminal charges; his wife, Gu Kailai, was convicted of murder in August.

2012-09-30.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-09-30.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.
